analyzers.description: Aggregated domain-aware semantic checks for SattLine programs and libraries

analyzers.description: Code-like content inside comments

analyzers.name: Variable shadowing

analyzers.name: Engineering spec compliance

analyzers.description: Detect dependency loops across sorted equation blocks and active step code
analyzers.name: Loop output refactor

analyzers.description: Cross-module duplicate tag, duplicate condition, priority, and latch-style alarm checks
analyzers.name: Alarm integrity

analyzers.description: Detect recipe and engineering parameter modules that do not resolve a required startup value
analyzers.name: Initial value validation

analyzers.description: Detect missing required parameter mappings, unknown targets, and cross-module contract mismatches
analyzers.name: Interface contracts

analyzers.description: Detect startup-value gaps and unsafe startup defaults that affect power-up behavior
analyzers.name: Power-up

analyzers.description: Detect inconsistent naming styles for variables, modules, and instances across the analyzed target
analyzers.name: Naming consistency

analyzers.description: Detect modules and SFC steps whose control-flow complexity exceeds default thresholds
analyzers.name: Cyclomatic complexity

analyzers.description: Detect moduletype instances whose resolved literal parameter values drift across the analyzed target
analyzers.name: Parameter drift

analyzers.description: Track reads before writes and signals that are written but never consumed
analyzers.name: Signal lifecycle

analyzers.description: Detect contradictory literal setpoint writes that can destabilize scan-loop behavior
analyzers.name: Loop stability

analyzers.description: Detect raised alarm or fault paths that are never cleared or never consumed
analyzers.name: Fault handling

analyzers.description: Validate literal assignments against visible Min/Max style bounds
analyzers.name: Numeric constraints

analyzers.description: Report deterministic dependency chains and initialization-order hazards
analyzers.name: Data dependency

analyzers.description: Detect moduletype instances whose visible configuration signatures drift across the analyzed target
analyzers.name: Config drift

analyzers.description: Detect non precision-scan-safe builtin calls inside equation blocks and SFC active code
analyzers.name: Scan-loop resource usage

analyzers.description: Detect unreleased or prematurely released resource handles and scan-loop resource hazards
analyzers.name: Resource usage

analyzers.description: Detect parallel scan or sequence branches that write the same variable without arbitration
analyzers.name: Scan concurrency

analyzers.description: Detect non-STATE variables that are read and written across multiple continuous scan sites
analyzers.name: Scan shared access

analyzers.description: Detect same-scan shared-variable hazards across modules and parallel SFC branches
analyzers.name: Same-cycle hazards

analyzers.description: Detect scan-cycle temporal hazards and non precision-scan-safe resource usage
analyzers.name: Timing

analyzers.description: Detect repeated module names that have drifted structurally beyond datecode-only changes
analyzers.name: Version drift

analyzers.description: Cross-module tracing for shutdown and emergency signal propagation
analyzers.name: Safety paths

analyzers.description: Cross-module taint tracing from external inputs to safety-critical sinks
analyzers.name: Taint paths

analyzers.description: Explicit boolean defaults that can enable logic or bypass safeguards at startup
analyzers.name: Unsafe defaults

analyzers.description: Constant-condition and unreachable-path detection across branches
analyzers.name: Lightweight dataflow

analyzers.description: Infer stable boolean and numeric state and report contradictory control flow
analyzers.name: State inference

rules.description: Conditions that always evaluate to false at a particular program point.
rules.explanation: Conditions that always evaluate to false at a particular program point.

rules.description: Conditions that always evaluate to true at a particular program point.
rules.explanation: Conditions that always evaluate to true at a particular program point.

rules.description: The same alarm tag or condition should not be configured with conflicting priorities or severities.
rules.explanation: The same alarm tag or condition should not be configured with conflicting priorities or severities.

rules.description: Parameter mappings whose connected datatypes are incompatible across module boundaries.
rules.explanation: Parameter mappings whose connected datatypes are incompatible across module boundaries.

rules.description: Writes whose value is overwritten before any later read can observe it.
rules.explanation: Writes whose value is overwritten before any later read can observe it.

rules.description: Alarm sources should not reuse the same condition without deliberate deduplication.
rules.explanation: Alarm sources should not reuse the same condition without deliberate deduplication.

rules.description: Alarm sources should not reuse the same tag across the analyzed target.
rules.explanation: Alarm sources should not reuse the same tag across the analyzed target.

rules.description: Sibling modules with the same case-insensitive name.
rules.explanation: Sibling modules with the same case-insensitive name.

rules.description: Transitions in the same sequence branch that normalize to the same guard logic.
rules.explanation: Transitions in the same sequence branch that normalize to the same guard logic.

rules.description: Complex datatypes that appear duplicated by structure.
rules.explanation: Complex datatypes that appear duplicated by structure.

rules.description: External MES, operator, or sensor inputs that propagate into safety-critical sinks across modules.
rules.explanation: External MES, operator, or sensor inputs that propagate into safety-critical sinks across modules.

rules.description: Fault paths that are raised should also be explicitly cleared or acknowledged in the same scope.
rules.explanation: Fault paths that are raised should also be explicitly cleared or acknowledged in the same scope.

rules.description: Fault paths that are raised should be consumed by reachable handling logic in the same scope.
rules.explanation: Fault paths that are raised should be consumed by reachable handling logic in the same scope.

rules.description: Root globals whose access never escapes a single module subtree and can be localized.
rules.explanation: Root globals whose access never escapes a single module subtree and can be localized.

rules.description: Root globals that act as implicit interfaces across multiple module paths.
rules.explanation: Root globals that act as implicit interfaces across multiple module paths.

rules.description: Root globals that are read or written by many distinct module paths and become highly shared coordination points.
rules.explanation: Root globals that are read or written by many distinct module paths and become highly shared coordination points.

rules.description: Configured mutually exclusive SFC steps can become active at the same time.
rules.explanation: Configured mutually exclusive SFC steps can become active at the same time.

rules.description: Boolean values that are set on some branches or steps without a matching False write on the complementary path.
rules.explanation: Boolean values that are set on some branches or steps without a matching False write on the complementary path.

rules.description: Instances of the same moduletype should not drift on mapped configuration parameter values without intent.
rules.explanation: Instances of the same moduletype should not drift on mapped configuration parameter values without intent.

rules.description: :OLD and :NEW access is only valid on variables or leaf fields declared State.
rules.explanation: :OLD and :NEW access is only valid on variables or leaf fields declared State.

rules.description: Sibling module invocations and rectangular graph or interact objects should not overlap in the same layout scope.
rules.explanation: Sibling module invocations and rectangular graph or interact objects should not overlap in the same layout scope.

rules.description: The same setpoint should not receive conflicting literal assignments in one scope.
rules.explanation: The same setpoint should not receive conflicting literal assignments in one scope.

rules.description: Min_/Max_ parameter mappings that do not line up by base name.
rules.explanation: Min_/Max_ parameter mappings that do not line up by base name.

rules.description: Recipe and engineering parameter modules should resolve a startup value through a default or an explicitly initialized mapping.
rules.explanation: Recipe and engineering parameter modules should resolve a startup value through a default or an explicitly initialized mapping.

rules.description: Configured SFC steps should initialize their required state in the enter block.
rules.explanation: Configured SFC steps should initialize their required state in the enter block.

rules.description: Configured SFC steps should clean up their required state in the exit block.
rules.explanation: Configured SFC steps should clean up their required state in the exit block.

rules.description: Case-insensitive declaration name collisions within a scope.
rules.explanation: Case-insensitive declaration name collisions within a scope.

rules.description: Role-bearing variable prefixes or suffixes such as Cmd, Status, and Alarm should align with observed read and write behavior.
rules.explanation: Role-bearing variable prefixes or suffixes such as Cmd, Status, and Alarm should align with observed read and write behavior.

rules.description: Alarm variables that are only forced true and never explicitly cleared can latch unexpectedly.
rules.explanation: Alarm variables that are only forced true and never explicitly cleared can latch unexpectedly.

rules.description: Datatype fields that are written but never subsequently read for a specific variable instance.
rules.explanation: Datatype fields that are written but never subsequently read for a specific variable instance.

rules.description: Variables that are written but never subsequently read.
rules.explanation: Variables that are written but never subsequently read.

rules.description: Assignments should stay within the visible Min_/Max_ bounds declared for the target variable.
rules.explanation: Assignments should stay within the visible Min_/Max_ bounds declared for the target variable.

rules.description: Parallel SFC branches that both read and write the same variable within one scan.
rules.explanation: Parallel SFC branches that both read and write the same variable within one scan.

rules.description: Parallel SFC branches that write to the same variable or field.
rules.explanation: Parallel SFC branches that write to the same variable or field.

rules.description: Procedure status outputs should be checked in control logic instead of being ignored or shown only in UI.
rules.explanation: Procedure status outputs should be checked in control logic instead of being ignored or shown only in UI.

rules.description: Variable reads that can occur before any definite assignment on the current path.
rules.explanation: Variable reads that can occur before any definite assignment on the current path.

rules.description: Datatype fields that are read but never written for a specific variable instance.
rules.explanation: Datatype fields that are read but never written for a specific variable instance.

rules.description: Writable declarations that are only ever read.
rules.explanation: Writable declarations that are only ever read.

rules.description: Module instances that leave internally used moduletype parameters unmapped.
rules.explanation: Module instances that leave internally used moduletype parameters unmapped.

rules.description: Reset-state writes that are missing or incomplete across sequence flow.
rules.explanation: Reset-state writes that are missing or incomplete across sequence flow.

rules.description: Non-STATE variables that are read and written across multiple continuous scan sites within the same scan.
rules.explanation: Non-STATE variables that are read and written across multiple continuous scan sites within the same scan.

rules.description: Shared variables that are read and written across multiple module paths within the same scan.
rules.explanation: Shared variables that are read and written across multiple module paths within the same scan.

rules.description: Implicit same-scan reads of a state variable after a write should use :NEW when they rely on the updated value.
rules.explanation: Implicit same-scan reads of a state variable after a write should use :NEW when they rely on the updated value.

rules.description: :OLD reads after a same-scan write where the expression still relies on the previous-scan snapshot.
rules.explanation: :OLD reads after a same-scan write where the expression still relies on the previous-scan snapshot.

rules.description: :OLD references are read-only and cannot be used as write targets or out-parameters.
rules.explanation: :OLD references are read-only and cannot be used as write targets or out-parameters.

rules.description: Conditions that compare a symbol to itself and collapse to a constant result.
rules.explanation: Conditions that compare a symbol to itself and collapse to a constant result.

rules.description: Local declarations that shadow outer or global names.
rules.explanation: Local declarations that shadow outer or global names.

rules.description: Signals should not be consumed before any definite write in the current scope.
rules.explanation: Signals should not be consumed before any definite write in the current scope.

rules.description: Signals that are written in a scope should be consumed later in that same scope.
rules.explanation: Signals that are written in a scope should be consumed later in that same scope.

rules.description: Configured SFC steps can inherit stale state from earlier steps when required enter writes are missing.
rules.explanation: Configured SFC steps can inherit stale state from earlier steps when required enter writes are missing.

rules.description: Parameter mappings whose string-like datatypes are incompatible.
rules.explanation: Parameter mappings whose string-like datatypes are incompatible.

rules.description: Transitions with guards that simplify to false and therefore can never fire.
rules.explanation: Transitions with guards that simplify to false and therefore can never fire.

rules.description: Transitions with guards that simplify to true and therefore always fire when reached.
rules.explanation: Transitions with guards that simplify to true and therefore always fire when reached.

rules.description: Variables that are only consumed through graphics or interact display wiring.
rules.explanation: Variables that are only consumed through graphics or interact display wiring.

rules.description: Safety-critical signals that are written but never consumed across the analyzed target.
rules.explanation: Safety-critical signals that are written but never consumed across the analyzed target.

rules.description: Unexpected non-module nodes under the submodule tree.
rules.explanation: Unexpected non-module nodes under the submodule tree.

rules.description: Parameter mappings that target undeclared module parameters.
rules.explanation: Parameter mappings that target undeclared module parameters.

rules.description: Branches that cannot execute because control-flow facts make them impossible.
rules.explanation: Branches that cannot execute because control-flow facts make them impossible.

rules.description: Sequence nodes that cannot execute because an earlier node terminates the branch.
rules.explanation: Sequence nodes that cannot execute because an earlier node terminates the branch.

rules.description: Sequence nodes that cannot execute after an earlier terminating node in the same branch.
rules.explanation: Sequence nodes that cannot execute after an earlier terminating node in the same branch.

rules.description: Transitions that can never fire because an earlier node structurally terminates the branch.
rules.explanation: Transitions that can never fire because an earlier node structurally terminates the branch.

rules.description: Boolean defaults that enable logic or bypass safeguards from startup.
rules.explanation: Boolean defaults that enable logic or bypass safeguards from startup.

rules.description: Datatype fields that are never used across the analyzed target.
rules.explanation: Datatype fields that are never used across the analyzed target.

rules.description: Declared variables that are never read or written.
rules.explanation: Declared variables that are never read or written.

rules.description: Variables whose written values are read internally but never reach a root-visible output.
rules.explanation: Variables whose written values are read internally but never reach a root-visible output.

rules.description: BasePicture code must stay inside frame modules.
rules.explanation: BasePicture code must stay inside frame modules.
rules.suggestion: Rename, reconnect, or restructure the affected construct so it matches the engineering specification.
